I see your point, but I have a concern.
If we add this feature, we will miss the deadline.
If the vendor is late, we may need a backup plan.
If we cut the scope, the client will not be happy.
If you agree, I will update the plan today.
Hi Rafael, I really want the login feature in this sprint. Can we add it now?
I see your point, Sandra, but I have a concern. If we add it now, we will miss the milestone on Friday.
That is a fair objection. But the client is waiting for it. What do you suggest?
As a compromise, if we move it to the next sprint, we will protect the deadline and still deliver it.
That is a reasonable trade-off. If you send a short summary, I will share it with the client today.
Perfect. I am glad we align on this. I will send the summary in one hour.
I see your point, but I have a concern about the deadline.
If we add it now, we will miss the milestone.
My objection is the timing, not the feature itself.
As a compromise, could we move it to the next sprint?
I am glad we align. If you agree, I will update the plan today.

Hi, this is Rafael. I am recording a quick note about the planning call this afternoon. The product owner wants to add a new login feature to the current sprint. I understand the idea, but I have a concern about the deadline. If we add this feature now, we will miss the milestone on Friday. There is also a trade-off with quality, because the team will not have time to test it well. My objection is not the feature itself. My objection is the timing. So in the call I will see her point first, then I will explain the constraint, and then I will propose a compromise. If we move the feature to the next sprint, we will protect the deadline and still deliver it. I want us to align on the plan before we talk to the client. I will keep my tone calm and professional, and I will not just say no. I will justify my position with clear reasons and offer a reasonable option.

Hi Rafael, this is Sandra. Thank you for your note about the new feature. I see your point about the deadline, and I share your concern. You are right that if we add it now, we will probably miss Friday. I do not want to break the milestone either. Here is my assumption, and please correct me if I am wrong: the client mainly wants the feature before the end of the month, not this exact week. If that is true, your compromise works for me. Let us move the login feature to the next sprint, protect the deadline, and tell the client the new date with a clear reason. I think that is a fair and reasonable trade-off. Could you prepare a short summary of the decision, so we both align on it before the client call? If you send it today, I will review it tonight. Thank you for raising this early and so professionally.
